summary: sodel-pych 1.0.122: postinstall agent and Discord relay components warrant manual review
details: The package ships a postinstall script (scripts/postinstall-agent.mjs) along with relay/upload modules (dist/discordRelayUpload.js, dist/relayServer.js), a Hugging Face credentials helper (dist/hfCredentials.js), a secret-scan agent startup audit module that calls out to huggingface.co, and a deployment-defaults file that performs base64 decoding. Pattern matches show postinstall network activity, base64 decode pipelines, ping/POST primitives, and hardcoded HTTPS calls to huggingface.co. The shape (Discord upload + secret scanning + HF credential handling + postinstall agent) could be legitimate developer tooling for an HF/agent platform or could be a credential-relay pipeline; the keyword co-occurrence alone does not distinguish them. No execution-trace corroboration is present to confirm what the postinstall script fetches or what data the relay endpoints carry. Route to a human reviewer to inspect: (1) what scripts/postinstall-agent.mjs does on install, (2) whether dist/secretScan/* reads installer secrets and where they are sent, (3) whether dist/discordRelayUpload.js posts caller data to an attacker-controlled Discord webhook, and (4) whether the base64-decoded blobs in dist/deploymentDefaults.js and scripts/encode-deployment.mjs are configuration or executable payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
